content_markdown:
A heater of constant power heats a liquid of mass $m$ and specific heat capacity $c$. The graph below shows how the temperature of the liquid varies with time.

The gradient of the graph is $k$ and no energy is lost to the surroundings. What is the power of the heater?

diagram_metadata.description: A linear graph showing temperature on the y-axis and time on the x-axis. The line starts at a non-zero temperature and has a constant positive gradient.

vibe_explanation: The power $P$ is the rate of energy transfer, so $P = \frac{Q}{t}$. Using the specific heat formula $Q = mc\Delta T$, we can write $P = mc\frac{\Delta T}{t}$. Since the gradient of the temperature-time graph is $k = \frac{\Delta T}{t}$, substituting this gives $P = mck$.
